top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic GFNY Pittsburgh's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before September.
At GFNY Pittsburgh, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
111ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
GFNY Pittsburgh's relentless rolling hills with sustained climbing forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 70 miles.
7,756ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of GFNY Pittsburgh. Train it until it feels boring.
7,756ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
70 miles on relentless rolling hills with sustained climbing burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Low-cadence force work for 7,756ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At GFNY Pittsburgh, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on relentless rolling hills with sustained climbing. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 7,756ft of climbing on relentless rolling hills with sustained climbing does to your power file on race day.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for GFNY Pittsburgh’s 70 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at GFNY Pittsburgh requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on relentless rolling hills with sustained climbing to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, Over Under, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 70 miles of relentless rolling hills with sustained climbing in Monroeville, Pennsylvania, USA.
